home.bio_p1: I'm a graduate electrical engineer at RED Engineering Design in London, working on large-scale data centre and power infrastructure projects. I hold a First Class MEng from the University of Bristol, specialising in energy and renewables.
home.bio_p2: At university I designed and helped build a solar DC microgrid for a remote community in the Brazilian Amazon, and independently completed a wind-solar mini-grid feasibility study awarded 83% and used as a cohort exemplar.
home.bio_p3: I grew up in the West Country, spent two years travelling before starting work, picking up many experiences along the way. Notably; working at a dog shelter in The Sacred Valley in Peru, a multi-levelled bar/pub in Melbourne, sporting events across the UK, horse races, festivals, including helping with the build and break for Glastonbury Festival. I came to believe the best things come from getting outside, doing things that scare you a bit, and paying attention to the world around you.
home.fact_currently: Graduate Electrical Engineer, RED Engineering Design

home.fact_interests: Renewable energy, lead climbing, bouldering, martial arts, travel, reading

life.hobby_climbing_desc: Outdoor and indoor. The problem-solving side keeps me coming back Ã¢ÂÂ route reading, body position, committing to moves you're not sure about.
life.hobby_bouldering_desc: Short, hard problems with no rope. Good for when you want to train without the faff. The London wall scene is solid.
life.hobby_martial_desc: The discipline matters as much as the physical Ã¢ÂÂ showing up consistently, being comfortable being a beginner again.
life.hobby_reading_desc: Broadly across science, technology, and fiction. More interested in ideas than genres Ã¢ÂÂ anything that makes me think differently.
life.hobby_travel_desc: Two years of it before starting work. Still the thing I think about most when planning what comes next.
life.hobby_energy_desc: Professionally and personally. The intersection of engineering, economics, and politics around energy transition is endlessly interesting.
life.project_grad_desc: An idea for helping engineering graduates navigate job applications Ã¢ÂÂ CV tailoring, curated job lists, interview prep. The market for new graduates is messier than it should be and most advice is generic.

travel.hero_sub: North America, Asia, Australia, Japan, Africa, Europe, South America. It changed my perspective; the contrasts in how people live, what communities really face, and how different much of the world is to back home.
